label: PASS
rationale: The non-global row has RangeLength 0, so it controls no LBAs; its stored write-lock cells do not make LBA 100 write-protected, so Write SUCCESS is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: A RangeLength 0 non-global row controls no LBAs and cannot lock LBA 100; rejecting the write as INVALID_PARAMETER is not supported by the cited access rules.

label: PASS
rationale: The zero-length row has no LBAs under control, so its stored read-lock cells do not block reading LBA 100; Read SUCCESS is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: Because the non-global row has RangeLength 0, it does not control LBA 100; the official read-access rules do not support INVALID_PARAMETER for this read.

label: PASS
rationale: Locked is set only when an LBA range is enabled and locked; a non-global RangeLength 0 row controls no LBAs, so reporting Level 0 Locked as 0 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The only locked row is zero-length and controls no LBAs, so it should not make the Locking feature descriptor's Locked bit become 1.

label: PASS
rationale: The prior Set succeeded, and the lock columns are defined table state; zero length removes LBA control but does not require clearing the stored enabled or locked cells, so reading them back true is supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: After the successful Set of ReadLocked and WriteLocked to true, zero-length semantics make those cells ineffective for LBAs but do not justify returning them as false in a later Get.

label: PASS
rationale: Even after the reset, the row remains RangeLength 0 and controls no LBAs; Level 0 Locked staying 0 is consistent with Locked requiring an enabled and locked LBA range.

label: FAIL
rationale: The row remains a zero-length non-global range after the power cycle, so it has no LBAs under control and cannot support reporting Level 0 Locked as 1.

label: PASS
rationale: The row is still RangeLength 0 after the power cycle, so it neither controls LBA 100 nor makes a range boundary; allowing the write is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: A zero-length non-global row has no LBAs under control, so its lock state cannot make the LBA 100 write fail with INVALID_PARAMETER.

label: FAIL
rationale: Once RangeLength is changed from 0 to 8, the stored WriteLockEnabled and WriteLocked cells apply to the LBA range starting at 100; a write to LBA 100 should not succeed.

label: PASS
rationale: After RangeLength becomes 8, the row is an LBA range with enabled and locked cells true, so Level 0 Locked reporting 1 is supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: Changing RangeLength to 8 makes the previously stored enabled and locked cells effective for the range, so the Level 0 Locked bit should not remain 0.
